Got a great topic for today. Listen guys, I gotta tell you two things about success here. Two words. Ready? Beliefs and beliefs. That's it. I had a great revelation myself. I was interviewing my head of sales, Matt Hirschberg. Great guy, incredibly successful. Has an amazing past story where he was dealing oxycontin. He went to jail. He lost a hundred pounds, became a motivational speaker, built a huge health chain, a health club chain, right? I mean, the guy's unbelievable, right? Now he's helping me build my business and he's just, he's an amazing guy, right? And you know, what it came down to from Matt and what I've said to you about my own life so many times, there's one, to someone else's lens here, is that he was in jail. He felt hopeless. He felt despair. He was desperate. He was negative. He was depressed. He was on medications to try to stop his depression, going from drug to drug to drug. And nothing helped him until finally he got so fed up with his own misery, with his own wallowing and self-pity, his own, his own life, what it has become, his physique, his lack of freedom being in jail. His just self, the whole way he carried himself, the way he viewed the world. He got so sick of it. It was so much pain, he felt. And he had this moment of clarity and he made a shift. He made a commitment that that's it. I'm done, never again, no more. I'm changing it. From that moment, everything stemmed. It was a single moment in time that changed everything from Matt. So fed up, so much pain that he made that shift. You know what, guys? It's always the case with people, when you're in a bad position, even if you're in an okay position, we have this tendency that we always get into this sort of position where we are as a nurse, we don't want to move forward. We're in our comfort zone and nothing can move us out of that. It's this extreme amount of energy to nudge you forward. Well, that energy can come from the outside sometimes, but most of all, it comes from in you, in the form of just like, I am so sick of feeling sick. I'm so sick of feeling like, shit, I'm done. I'm not going to take it anymore. I see when change happens, it happens in a heartbeat. It doesn't take a long time. When you make the shift, it does not take, it's not this long, slow slog of a situation where you just drag and drag in the muck and mire trying to get to this. Well, it's not what happens. What happens is, is that you get caught in the muck and mire of negativity and you stay there and you stay there and you stay there and you don't really move within all of it. Then finally, you get to this point where you're so fed up, you're done, you just can't take it. It's no mass. I can't take anymore. And in that moment, you make a commitment to change. And from there, that's when all things flow. You get it, guys? It's up to you. No, listen, you have the ability at any moment in time to turn your life in any direction you want. That's what the human beings, we got that gift. We're not creatures of circumstance, we're creators of circumstances. But here's the deal. To do that, you have to harness the power of your own psychology, your own self-talk. What are you saying to them? What are you thinking about? Listen, you actually can, you can accelerate this process by getting really clear about how much your life sucks. Stop minimizing it. Stop rationalizing it. Step into the misery and literally let yourself experience the misery to the absolute fullest. Don't try to minimize it. And then just, and then just link to that. All the people that you're hurting, all the misery that you bring on, those that you love to make yourself feel the pain. That's the key. Because once you do that, see, there's this certain threshold of pain that once you cross over it, it's like you just can't take anymore. And in that moment, that's where greatness lies. Because you're, you have this almost as ricochet effect. Because your life is so, it's like you're stretching a rubber band. You stretch your rubber band, or you're so negative, you're so negative, you're so negative, you're so negative. It's like you're stretched out all the way to your ready to break. Well, guess what? In that stretch, there's a lot of stored energy in there. So when you finally release that, it goes to, bam, sky rockets back the other way to massive greatness. The worst of pain, the greater the ride back up to greatness. You understand? In other words, you just power in your pain, this power in your misery, this power in failure, if you learn how to harness it, it starts by not trying to minimize it. Step into it. Let it be though, you have to destroy you emotionally, literally to the point you're so, you're so miserable over it. You know, you know, that's it. I'm done. I can't live this way anymore. I can't exist this way anymore. I can't bring this shit in my family anymore. And once that happens, when you have that much pain, guess what happens? Your brain says, I have to change. I have to make a shift. Because this ain't working. It's when you try to convince yourself that it's not that bad. I'm still okay. There's still hope. You know, just look, you know, honestly, there's not if you're going in a really bad, if you're in a dead end job, if you're not making ends meet, if you're not feeling good, you know what? It's not getting you know, the definition of insanity, classic Albert Einstein definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over again, expecting a different result. What the fuck do you think is going to happen if you keep on the same course, the same result? Nothing's going to change. It changes when you say it's time to change. And then you take all that store energy and you skyrocket success. Now, that being said, there's work to do. You have to grow yourself, make yourself a better business person, a better closer. But you're doing that at the same time, you're educating yourself, you're growing yourself, you're getting strong, right? It's you know, you're exiting the winter of your own life now, and it's spring, you're ready to start to grow up, you get to get strong and with your high brain get strong, learn yourself, grow yourself, make sure that when you're ready to make the shift that you're going to boom, you have that ability to take the power. So you have to take that power and direct it. And the way you directed is by having the skill sets that direct it. You get it? It's a very simple formula. Okay, it's desire, directed energy with the right skills. You have desires, you direct energy, but with the right skills. The skills that's the wrong doesn't matter. When I was trying to write my book until I learned how to write, how to strategy, write, get it back, work 10,000 years, I would never write a good book. But by learning the skills, and that's why the straight line is so crucial because it teaches you that skill how to close, how to communicate, how to get your point of course, how to get what you want in life. That combined with the desire and the pain, harnessing the power of pain, boy, I'm telling you, you can make that change anytime you want. And that doesn't mean just people who are totally negative in the shitter right now.
